entities.name: Bóng cờ vua
entities.description: Bóng trắng được sử dụng để đánh trong trò chơi bàng cờ vua.

entities.name: Bóng đối tượng
entities.description: 15 bóng có số từ 1 đến 15 được sử dụng trong trò chơi bàng cờ vua.

entities.name: Bảng cờ vua
entities.description: Một bàn hình chữ nhật với sáu hố - một ở mỗi góc và một ở giữa mỗi hàng dài.

entities.name: Sợi bọc
entities.description: Sợi bọc được làm từ vải lông và nylon, thường là màu xanh lam hoặc xanh lá cây trong các trận đấu chuyên nghiệp.

entities.name: Bóng 8-ball
entities.description: Bóng đen có số 8 và được coi là bóng quan trọng nhất trên bàn cờ vua.

entities.name: Bóng 9-ball
entities.description: Trò chơi bàng cờ vua với chỉ sử dụng các bóng từ số 1 đến 9.

entities.name: Bóng 10-ball
entities.description: Trò chơi bàng cờ vua với chỉ sử dụng các bóng từ số 1 đến 10.

entities.name: Cue Ball

entities.name: Object Ball

entities.name: Solid Ball

entities.name: Stripe Ball
entities.description: One of the colored balls in a group of similar color in billiards.

entities.name: 8-ball

entities.name: 9-ball

entities.name: 10-ball

entities.name: racking
entities.description: The process of arranging the balls on the table before play begins.

entities.name: foot spot
entities.description: A designated spot on the table where the front ball is placed.

entities.description: A geometric shape with three sides used for racking balls in 8-ball and 10-ball.

entities.name: diamond
entities.description: A geometric shape with four sides used for racking balls in 9-ball.

entities.name: Lag

entities.name: Head string

entities.name: Foot cushion

entities.description: A specific game variant of billiards.

entities.name: stripes
entities.description: The other group of balls in 8-ball billiards, consisting of numbered balls from 9 to 15.

entities.name: break shot

entities.name: Break shot

entities.description: The white ball used to strike other balls in billiards.

entities.name: Head string
entities.description: A line on the table that marks where the cue ball should be placed for a break shot.

entities.name: Rack

entities.name: Object balls
entities.description: Balls other than the cue ball and 8-ball used in billiards.

entities.name: Cushions
entities.description: The sides of the table that balls can hit during play.

entities.name: 8-ball
entities.description: A colored ball used in 8-ball billiards.

entities.name: Ball in hand
entities.description: The incoming player's option to start shooting from behind the head string after a foul.

entities.name: Break
entities.description: The initial shot at the start of a game in billiards.

entities.name: Open Table
entities.description: A state where neither player has been assigned solids or stripes after the break.

entities.name: Solids
entities.description: The numbered balls in billiards, typically 1 through 7.

entities.name: Stripes
entities.description: The colored balls in billiards, typically 9 through 15.

entities.name: Group Assignment
entities.description: The process of assigning a group (solids or stripes) to a player based on their first legal shot after the break.

entities.name: 8-ball
entities.description: A billiard ball used in 8-ball pool.

entities.name: shooter's group
entities.description: The set of balls a player is trying to pocket first in 8-ball pool.

entities.name: pocket
entities.description: A hole on the billiard table where balls can be pocketed.

entities.name: foul
entities.description: An illegal action in billiards that results in a penalty.

entities.name: Combination shots
entities.description: A legal shot in 8-ball billiards where a player uses any ball on the table to create a combination, including opponent's balls or the 8-ball.

entities.name: Cue ball
entities.description: The white ball used by players in billiards to strike other balls.

entities.name: Solid balls
entities.description: A group of numbered balls (1-7) in 8-ball billiards.

entities.name: Striped balls
entities.description: A group of numbered balls (9-15) in 8-ball billiards.

entities.name: 8-ball
entities.description: The black ball used as the target ball in 8-ball billiards.

entities.name: 8-ball
entities.description: A variant of billiards where players use numbered balls to achieve specific objectives.

entities.name: ball in hand
entities.description: A penalty in 8-ball where the incoming player is given a free shot with the cue ball on the table.

entities.name: cue ball
entities.description: The primary striking ball used in billiards, including 8-ball.

entities.name: legal ball
entities.description: A ball that can be legally hit during a shot in 8-ball.

entities.name: cushion
entities.description: The boundary of the table where balls can pocketed or bounce off.

entities.name: pocketed
entities.description: A foul in 8-ball where a ball is accidentally hit into one of the pockets.

entities.name: double hit
entities.description: A foul in 8-ball where the cue ball is struck more than once during a shot.

entities.name: push shot
entities.description: A foul in 8-ball where the cue ball is pushed instead of being struck with the cue stick.

entities.name: touching or moving any ball with body, clothing, or cue
entities.description: A foul in 8-ball where a player touches or moves a ball with their body, clothing, or cue during a shot.

entities.name: shooting out of turn
entities.description: A foul in 8-ball where a player shoots when it is not their turn to do so.

entities.name: placing the cue ball in the wrong area on a ball-in-hand situation
entities.description: A foul in 8-ball where the cue ball is placed in an incorrect position during a ball-in-hand situation.

entities.name: Foul

entities.name: cue ball

entities.description: The ball with the lowest number on it that must be contacted first when shooting.

entities.name: Break shot
entities.description: The initial shot made by a player at the start of a game.

entities.name: Push out
entities.description: A strategy where a player hits the cue ball in any direction without contacting the lowest numbered ball first or driving any ball to a cushion immediately after the break shot.

entities.name: cue ball
entities.description: The white ball used to strike other balls in billiards.

entities.name: head string
entities.description: A line on the table that marks where the cue ball should be placed for a break shot.

entities.name: 1-ball

entities.name: object balls
entities.description: The numbered balls used in billiards besides the cue ball and the 9-ball.

entities.name: cushions
entities.description: The sides of the table that the balls can hit during play.

entities.name: break shot
entities.description: The initial shot made by the player who has possession of the cue ball at the start of a game or after a foul.

entities.name: 9-ball

entities.name: Breaker
entities.description: Player who attempts to pocket balls on the break shot.

entities.name: 9-ball
entities.description: The highest-value ball in 9-ball billiards.

entities.name: Illegal Break
entities.description: Break shot that does not meet the required criteria for a successful break.

entities.name: Ball in Hand
entities.description: Position where the incoming player has the option to choose any ball on the table as their next shot.

entities.name: Foul
entities.description: A violation during gameplay that results in a penalty.

entities.description: The lowest numbered ball on the table that must be hit first during a shot.

entities.name: Pocket

entities.name: Foot Spot

entities.description: A variant of billiards where players use a cue ball and nine numbered balls.

entities.description: A popular game in billiards played with 9 balls.

entities.description: The white ball used to strike other balls in billiards.

entities.description: Shots that do not require a call due to their obvious nature.

entities.description: Unintentional pocketing of balls in 10-ball, which does not count towards the score.

entities.name: cue ball
entities.description: The primary ball used in billiards, hit by the cue stick to make shots.

entities.description: A mathematical pattern used in 3-cushion billiards where starting and ending diamonds add up to 2.

entities.description: Another mathematical pattern used in 3-cushion billiards for shorter rail travel, cross-table shots, and end-rail patterns.
